body:
`JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync('games-metadata.json'))` reads a Buffer by default; `JSON.parse` expects a string and this will throw at runtime. Read the file as UTF-8 (or call `.toString('utf8')`) before parsing.
```suggestion
const meta = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync('games-metadata.json', 'utf8'));
```

This script attempts to splice a code block into the HTML by concatenating raw (unquoted) JavaScript source; as written it’s invalid JS and won’t run. Store the inserted block as a properly quoted string (e.g., a template literal) before concatenation.

This replaces the entire Tetris implementation with a placeholder `console.log`, so the game no longer exists/functionally runs. This conflicts with the PR title (“recreate … games from scratch”) and the repository’s game spec expectations (see GAME_DEVELOPMENT_GUIDE) — please restore a playable Tetris implementation (10×20 board, tetrominoes, controls, scoring, restart, etc.).

This replaces the Minesweeper implementation with a placeholder `console.log`, so the gemini-3-1-pro Minesweeper version is no longer playable. Please restore the game logic and UI (grid, safe-first-click, flagging, win/loss handling) per the project’s game guide.

This replaces the Reversi implementation with a placeholder `console.log`, so the gemini-3-1-pro Reversi version is no longer playable. Please restore a full 8×8 Reversi game (valid-move highlighting, flipping, scoring, and AI opponent) per the project’s game guide.

This replaces the Endless Runner implementation with a placeholder `console.log`, so the gemini-3-1-pro Endless Runner version is no longer playable. Please restore a working game (player controls, obstacles, scoring, game-over + restart) consistent with the other versions and the repository’s expectations.

This replaces the 3D Maze implementation with a placeholder `console.log`, so the gemini-3-1-pro 3D Maze version is no longer playable. Please restore a functional maze game (movement controls, timer, win/lose conditions, restart).

The game-over detection ends the game whenever the board is full (`!b.includes(0)`) even if merges are still possible, which will prematurely stop valid games. Game-over should only trigger when the board is full *and* no adjacent equal tiles exist in any row/column; similarly, consider adding a win check when a 2048 tile is created.

This script is currently syntactically invalid: it attempts to concatenate a multi-line snippet without quoting/templating (e.g., `+ \// Laser collision`), and the trailing `\ + s.substring(p2)` is also not valid JS. As written, the script cannot run.

`ctx.fillStyle = hit==2 ? '#0f0' : \rgb(0,\\,\\);` is invalid JavaScript (and will throw a syntax error). Replace it with a valid color string (e.g., `'rgb(r,g,b)'`) or a computed value, and remove the stray backslashes/escape sequences.
```suggestion
        ctx.fillStyle = hit==2 ? '#0f0' : 'rgb(0,0,255)';
```

`spawn()` can place food on top of the snake because it doesn't avoid occupied cells. That can cause immediate “eats” or make food unreachable on longer snakes. Consider retrying until a free cell is found (or tracking free cells).

After a win, the code mutates the overlay title (`#over h1`) to `YOU WIN!`, but `st()` does not reset it back to `GAME OVER`. Subsequent losses can incorrectly show the win title. Reset the overlay heading in `st()` (or maintain separate win/lose screens).
```suggestion
        if(m) { run=false; document.getElementById('over').querySelector('h1').innerText='GAME OVER'; document.getElementById('over').style.display='flex'; document.getElementById('fs').innerText=sc; document.getElementById('st').innerText='OVER'; }
```
